title: AI is giving bad advice to flatter its users, says new study on dangers of overly agreeable chatbots
description: Finding a fix to AI’s emerging problems will be critical as society still grapples with the effects of social media technology after more than a decade of warnings from parents and child advocates.

title: Google Search Live AI Mode Goes Global, Expands to More Than 200 Countries
description: Google expands Search Live in AI Mode globally, enabling conversational, voice-driven search with camera support across 200+ countries and languages.

title: Ecosystem Roundup: Supply chain cyberattacks surge in SEA; Amity’s US$100M signals AI rise; US blocks Anthropic ban; Philippines cyber gap widens
description: Southeast Asia’s digital economy has grown at breakneck speed, but its cybersecurity posture is struggling to keep pace. title: WhatsApp rolls out AI features, cross-platform chat transfer and more
description: WhatsApp is rolling out a set of new features aimed at improving chat management, storage control and multi-account use. Among the updates is a new storage management option that lets users find and delete large files in individual chats without deleting entire conversations. Users can also choose to delete only media files while keeping text history intact. The platform has also expanded its chat transfer feature to support moving chat history between iOS and Android devices. The update is intended to simplify switching phones, allowing users to move conversations, photos and videos more easily. In another update, the platform is introducing support for using two accounts on a single iPhone, a feature previously available on Android. This allows users to manage personal and work accounts on one device. The platform is also adding AI-powered tools. Users can now edit photos within chats using Meta AI features, including options to remove objects, change backgrounds or apply styles. An updated writing assistant can also suggest responses based on conversation context, while the messages remain private. Additionally, WhatsApp will suggest stickers based on emojis typed by users, aiming to make it easier to express reactions in chats. These features are rolling out now and are expected to be available to everyone soon.
